claim: Chasing a lost dog — including any forward movement toward the animal, even by the owner — triggers the fight-or-flight response; the dog does not distinguish its owner from a predator during this state.
stance_target: Chasing is the primary owner behavior error that reduces recovery probability
scope_conditions: Applies to all lost dog profiles, but especially fear-based disappearances and skittish/traumatized dogs
quoted_support: you are a PREDATOR at that moment. A dog in fight-or-flight does not distinguish owner from predator.

claim: ATVs and motor vehicles approaching a scared lost dog trigger flight response before visual contact is made, because the sound of the engine reaches the dog first.
stance_target: Vehicle-based searches near a known sighting location can push the dog further away
scope_conditions: Applies to all lost dog recovery operations where location is partially known
quoted_support: ATVs/quads (motor sound triggers flight before visual contact)

claim: The lure protocol requires: get low/flat on ground, avoid eye contact, speak softly, allow the dog to approach on its own terms, do not reach — let the dog make physical contact first, multiple times if skittish.
stance_target: Passive luring is the operationally correct capture technique for frightened lost dogs
scope_conditions: Skittish and traumatized dogs; partially applicable to aloof dogs
quoted_support: get low/flat on ground immediately; avoid eye contact; speak softly/calmly; allow dog to approach on its own terms; do not reach — let dog touch first, multiple times if skittish.

claim: Effective food station baits include smelly wet canned food (ALPO with gravy), deli meats with liquid smoke or Worcestershire sauce, canned cat food, and boneless fried chicken — high olfactory value targets.
stance_target: Olfactory-rich baits are required for food stations because panicked dogs rely on smell over sight
scope_conditions: Food station setup for skittish or displaced dogs
quoted_support: smelly wet canned food (ALPO w/ gravy cited), deli meats, liquid smoke, Worcestershire, canned cat food, fried chicken (boneless).

claim: Lost pet posters and social media should withhold the dog's exact current location — sharing location publicly floods the area with well-meaning people who inadvertently push the dog further away.
stance_target: Sighting location management is a critical operational variable, not just a communications preference
scope_conditions: Applies from day 1 of search; especially important once a sighting pattern establishes a residence area
quoted_support: keep vague enough to verify sightings (don't give full location — prevents well-meaning people from mobbing the area and pushing the dog further). Social media: same 'just enough info' rule.

claim: Familiar scent articles (dirty laundry, dog bed) placed near the escape point help draw a lost dog back to a recognizable anchor before it has established a new territory.
stance_target: Scent anchor deployment is a standard early-phase recovery technique
scope_conditions: Phase 0–48h after escape, when dog is still near escape point
quoted_support: Familiar scent articles: dirty laundry, dog bed near escape point.
